title: galium andrewsii subsp. intermedium
intro: Galium andrewsii subsp. intermedium, also known as intermediate bedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium andrewsii plant, native to the western coast of North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and astringent. The health benefits of Galium andrewsii subsp. intermedium include its potential to treat urinary tract issues, menstrual cramps, and skin irritations, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate cold symptoms. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
